entries.name: RQ-11B Raven
entries.notes: Hand-launched, battery-powered. Standard US Army/Marine squad-level ISR.

entries.name: RQ-12A Wasp IV
entries.notes: Smallest SURSS UAS. Platoon/squad-level front-line recon.

entries.name: RQ-20B Puma AE
entries.notes: Largest SURSS. Company-level persistent ISR. Waterproof, land/sea recovery.

entries.name: Switchblade 300
entries.notes: Man-portable loitering munition. Anti-personnel focused blast warhead.

entries.name: Switchblade 600
entries.notes: Anti-armor loitering munition. Multi-purpose warhead, Javelin-class.

entries.name: PD-100 Black Hornet 3
entries.notes: Nano UAS for individual soldier. Virtually silent. GPS-denied capable.

entries.name: ScanEagle
entries.notes: Long-endurance Group 2 ISR. Catapult launched, SkyHook recovery.

entries.name: Modified FPV Racing Drone
entries.notes: Weaponized FPV drone. Common in Ukraine conflict. Carries grenades/shaped charges.

entries.name: Modified DJI Mavic (weaponized)
entries.notes: Commercial DJI drone modified as ISR/drop platform. Common threat profile.

entries.name: Unknown Commercial Drone (unclassified)
entries.notes: Default profile for unidentified commercial-class drone in AO.

entries.name: Shahed-136 (Geran-2)
entries.notes: Delta-wing loitering munition. ~40 kg warhead. Used extensively in Ukraine.

entries.name: Orlan-10
entries.notes: Russian tactical ISR/EW UAS. Can jam GPS, direct artillery. Common threat.
